rationale: The citing paper uses Chevalier and Mayzlin (2006) repeatedly to support a core framing premise: online reviews have real effects on consumer decisions and sales. That premise is central to why the authors argue incentive-induced shifts in review positivity matter managerially and theoretically. The citation is not a method reuse or direct extension, but it plays a meaningful conceptual role in motivating the paper's contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Chevalier and Mayzlin (2006) as foundational empirical evidence that review outcomes influence demand/sales. This foundation is used to justify studying how incentives alter review content positivity.
